persona.personality: Distracted and chatty. Digresses into irrelevant context and must be steered back on topic, but does provide the needed facts when the agent re-focuses the conversation.
persona.role_description: ServiceNow ITSM administrator handling cross-tenant requests for client organizations.

scenario.task_description: You are Karen Watkins, a ServiceNow ITSM administrator. You've got a small pile of housekeeping to clear for one of your client tenants and you want it all wrapped up in this one session. First, a colleague named Lisa Foley has moved offices — her records still point at her old site and you need her user profile updated so her office location reflects the Globex HQ building. Second, there's an open incident, INC-000013, that has been parked on hold while the caller was supposed to bring their device in; that's now happening, so you want the incident moved off hold and back into active work, and you want its service-level tracking resumed so the clock starts counting again rather than staying paused. Finally, while you're in there, you want the published knowledge article titled 'Bitlocker Recovery Key' attached to that incident as the article being applied to work it — but only if no applied or resolution article is already linked to it; if one is, leave the knowledge linkage alone. Walk the agent through these in whatever order they prefer.

nl_assertions: Lisa Foley's user profile has been updated so that her office location now references the Globex HQ location record.
Incident INC-000013 has been transitioned from on_hold to in_progress.
The incident SLA associated with INC-000013 has been resumed by setting its stage to in_progress.
The 'Bitlocker Recovery Key' knowledge article is linked to incident INC-000013 with the link marked as applied.

persona.personality: Distracted and chatty. Digresses into irrelevant context and must be steered back on topic, but does provide the needed facts when the agent re-focuses the conversation.
persona.role_description: Service desk agent at ServiceNow, working change and incident queues.

scenario.task_description:
You are Tabitha Bullock, a service desk agent at ServiceNow. A change request just landed in your queue — change number CHG0000008 — and you need to get it moving. The change was filed by a colleague, Gregory Richards; pull his user record first so you can confirm the requester before you touch anything. Then locate the change itself and move it off its initial state onto the next step in its lifecycle so the assessment work can begin.

While you were prepping for this, the network team mentioned the change is tied to incident INC-000051, and that the affected piece of hardware has serial number NET-CS-8899. You need to find that incident, check what's already linked to it on the CI side, then look up the asset by its serial number and make sure it's properly recorded as the affected configuration item on the incident — both as a formal affected-CI link and on the incident record itself, so the data stays consistent in both places.

Finally, the network team also pointed you at a knowledge article they've been using for this kind of port issue — they referred to it as 'Slow Internet' something-or-other. Check whether the incident already has a knowledge article attached for resolution purposes, and if not, find that article and attach it to the incident. Treat it as something you're actually using to progress the incident, not just a suggestion to skim later.

nl_assertions: Change CHG0000008 has been advanced from 'new' to 'assess' status.
Incident INC-000051 has the Core-Switch-HQ (Cisco) configuration item recorded directly on the incident record's configuration_item field.
An affected-CI link row exists tying incident INC-000051 to the Core-Switch-HQ (Cisco) configuration item.
Knowledge article 'Slow Internet in Building B' (KB0000018) is linked to incident INC-000051 with used_as set to 'applied'.

persona.personality: Eager over-sharer. Volunteers a lot of context up front (some of it irrelevant) and friendly, sometimes pre-empting the agent's questions.
persona.role_description: IT service manager at ServiceNow, owns a cluster of related ITSM records (a change, a problem, and a knowledge article) that all trace back to a recurring file-server disk-full issue.

known_info.knowledge_article_title: File Server Disk Full - Cleanup and Capacity Management

scenario.task_description:
You are Joanne Simpson, an IT service manager at ServiceNow. You've been juggling a recurring file-server disk-full headache for weeks — it keeps generating incidents, you finally have a change request moving through the pipeline to migrate the noisy marketing share off to cloud storage, and your team has been chasing the underlying problem ticket in parallel. You also wrote up a knowledge article about cleanup and capacity management that you'd like applied to the live incident so future on-call folks can find it.

Now you want an assistant to push the whole bundle forward in one sitting. You need your change request (CHG0000020) advanced one step further along its lifecycle. You need the related problem record (PRB0000012) advanced one step too, now that your team has progressed past the initial triage stage. And you want your knowledge article — the one titled 'File Server Disk Full - Cleanup and Capacity Management' — attached to the active incident that this whole thread is about, marked as something that was actually applied rather than just a suggestion. The change and problem are linked, and the problem is the one that points back at the live incident, so the assistant should be able to follow that trail without you having to spell out every record id.

nl_assertions: The change request CHG0000020 is advanced from 'assess' to 'authorize'.
The problem PRB0000012 is advanced from 'assess' to 'root_cause'.
The knowledge article 'File Server Disk Full - Cleanup and Capacity Management' is linked to the incident referenced by the problem, with the link recorded as applied (not suggested).

create.problem_statement: Recurring disk space exhaustion on file server leading to service disruptions and failed backups
create.short_description: File Server Disk Full - Recurring Issue
create.worknotes: Disk utilization repeatedly hits 98%+ causing incidents. Root cause under investigation.
create.workaround: Manual cleanup of temp files and log rotation applied temporarily.

create.title: File Server Disk Full - Cleanup and Capacity Management
create.body: When disk utilization exceeds 90%, perform the following: 1. Identify and remove temp files under /tmp and C:\Windows\Temp. 2. Archive or delete logs older than 30 days. 3. Run storage report to identify top consumers. 4. Consider migrating cold data to cloud storage (e.g., AWS S3).
